description: Etapa 3: Expense lifecycle dual-write. Cada despesa grava em mp_expenses E no event ledger via expense_captured/expense_classified/expense_exported events.

userStories.title: Expense event types + record_expense_event + derive_expense_status
userStories.description: As a system, I need 4 new expense lifecycle event types, a helper function to record them, and a status derivation function.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 event types added to EVENT_TYPES in app/services/event_ledger.py: expense_captured (any), expense_classified (zero), expense_reviewed (zero), expense_exported (zero)
Function record_expense_event(seller_slug, payment_id, event_type, signed_amount, competencia_date, expense_type, metadata=None) added
Idempotency key: {seller}:{payment_id}:{event_type} (3 parts)
ml_payment_id extracted via int(payment_id.split(':')[0]) with fallback 0
Metadata always includes expense_type key
source set to 'expense_lifecycle'

Function derive_expense_status(event_types: set[str]) -> str added: expense_exported->exported, expense_reviewed->reviewed, expense_classified->auto_categorized, expense_captured->pending_review, else->unknown
Unit tests for record_expense_event and derive_expense_status added
python3 -m pytest passes (161+ tests)
userStories.notes: Read rfcs/plano-execucao/04-fase2-dual-write.md for exact function signatures. Read app/services/event_ledger.py for existing patterns. The expense_captured event uses sign 'any' because it can be positive (income) or negative (expense).

userStories.title: Dual-write in expense_classifier.py
userStories.description: As a system, I need expense_classifier.py to write expense_captured (and expense_classified if auto-categorized) to the event ledger after upserting to mp_expenses.
acceptanceCriteria: In classify_non_order_payment(), AFTER the upsert to mp_expenses, call record_expense_event with event_type='expense_captured'
signed_amount: positive for income direction, negative for expense direction
competencia_date: extracted from date_approved or date_created ([:10] for date only)
If row is auto_categorized, also call record_expense_event with event_type='expense_classified' and metadata containing ca_category
Dual-write failure (EventRecordError) is logged as warning but does NOT prevent mp_expenses upsert

Metadata of expense_captured includes all fields needed for future Fase 3: expense_type, expense_direction, ca_category, auto_categorized, description, amount, date_created, date_approved, business_branch, operation_type, payment_method, external_reference, beneficiary_name, notes
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read app/services/expense_classifier.py fully. The classify_non_order_payment() function upserts to mp_expenses. Add the dual-write AFTER the successful upsert. CRITICAL: the metadata must contain ALL fields listed — without them, Fase 3 (migrate reads) will not have enough data to reconstruct responses.

userStories.title: Dual-write in extrato_ingester.py
userStories.description: As a system, I need extrato_ingester.py to write expense_captured to the event ledger after inserting to mp_expenses.
acceptanceCriteria: In ingest_extrato_for_seller(), AFTER the 'Insert new' block that upserts to mp_expenses, call record_expense_event with event_type='expense_captured'
Same signed_amount, competencia_date, and metadata pattern as expense_classifier dual-write
If auto_categorized, also write expense_classified event
Dual-write failure logged as warning, does NOT block mp_expenses insert
Also apply same dual-write in ingest_extrato_from_csv() if it inserts to mp_expenses
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read app/services/extrato_ingester.py. The insert to mp_expenses is in the 'Insert new' block around line 950. This is a large function (~360 lines) — be careful to add the dual-write at the correct location. Follow the same metadata pattern as US-002.

userStories.title: Dual-write in release_report_sync.py + export.py
userStories.description: As a system, I need release_report_sync.py to write expense_captured for payouts/cashback/shipping, and export.py to write expense_exported after marking rows as exported.
acceptanceCriteria: In release_report_sync.py sync_release_report(), AFTER inserting payouts/cashback/shipping to mp_expenses, call record_expense_event with event_type='expense_captured'
Same metadata pattern as previous dual-writes

In app/routers/expenses/export.py, AFTER marking rows as 'exported' in mp_expenses, loop through rows and call record_expense_event with event_type='expense_exported', signed_amount=0, metadata containing batch_id
Both dual-writes: failure logged as warning, does NOT block main operation
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Read app/services/release_report_sync.py for payout/cashback/shipping insert locations. Read app/routers/expenses/export.py for the export flow — the batch marking happens around line 181-191. The expense_exported event has signed_amount=0 (it's a lifecycle flag, not a financial amount).

userStories.title: Validate dual-write consistency
userStories.description: As an operator, I need to verify that every mp_expense has a corresponding expense_captured event in the ledger.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation SQL query works: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mp_expenses WHERE seller_slug = '141air' equals SELECT COUNT(*) FROM payment_events WHERE seller_slug = '141air' AND event_type = 'expense_captured'
Sum comparison: SUM(amount) from mp_expenses matches SUM(ABS(signed_amount)) from expense_captured for same seller
DRE unchanged: get_dre_summary for 141air jan2026 returns same values as before dual-write
Export ZIP: generates same content as before (still reads from mp_expenses)
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: This story is about running validation queries, not writing new code. Run the SQL via mcp__supabase__execute_sql. If counts don't match, investigate and fix the dual-write in previous stories.
